description: Clear the stale `system-cron` argv on the tmux server (#273) via a heartbeat date-gated, detached restart of the durable session stack. Operational maintenance — spec-execute runs the reviewed runbook-as-code; there is no code feature to merge.

userStories.title: Detached, idempotent tmux-server restart script
userStories.description: As the operator, I need a reviewed restart-as-code artifact that survives killing its own session, so the server argv is cleared without orphaning the durable session stack.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/maintenance/restart-openharness-tmux.sh captures the live durable session->(cwd,command) map before kill-server and replays it
It is safe to launch detached (setsid): trap '' HUP, flock single-instance, and a done-sentinel make re-runs a no-op
Relaunch order puts the website origin before its tunnel and cron-system before cron-watchdog; it never recreates the legacy `system-cron` session name
It clears a stale crons/.pid naming a dead runtime, verifies the six durable sessions + mifune.dev 200 + cleared argv, and closes #273 on success (comments + stays open if degraded)
userStories.notes: Authored + self-critiqued + adversarially reviewed pre-merge. Runs unattended at 2026-06-20 12:00 America/Denver.

userStories.title: Heartbeat date-gated spec-execute trigger
userStories.description: As the operator, I need the heartbeat to execute this spec once at the scheduled time, mirroring the #264 weekly-step pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: crons/heartbeat.md gains a one-shot step gated to TZ=America/Denver date 2026-06-20 hour 12, launching the script detached and touching a per-day sentinel so it fires once
The step preserves the existing heartbeat logging contract (locked-append, no raw >> crons/.cron.log)
The step is documented as the spec-execute node of tasks/restart-openharness-tmux/
userStories.notes: Date-gate, not day-of-week gate; auto-cleared after the date passes in a follow-up.
